system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

startTime       0;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writePrecision  6;

timeFormat      general;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linear;

}

laplacianSchemes

}

snGradSchemes

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-05;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

PISO
{
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    residualControl
    {
        U               1e-4;
        p               1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }

    relativeTolerance   0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>
<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pisoFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Unable to set reference cell for field p
    Please supply either pRefCell or pRefPoint


file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/Cavity/3/system/fvSolution/PISO from line 46 to line 56.

    From function bool Foam::setRefCell(const volScalarField&, const volScalarField&, const dictionary&, label&, scalar&, bool)
    in file cfdTools/general/findRefCell/findRefCell.C at line 102.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error message is:

```
Unable to set reference cell for field p
    Please supply either pRefCell or pRefPoint
```

This occurs during the PIPSO solve phase. For pressure-based solvers like `pisoFoam`, OpenFOAM needs a reference point or cell to anchor the pressure field (since pressure is defined up to a constant). Without it, the Poisson equation for pressure cannot be solved.

Looking at the current `fvSolution`, there is no `pRefCell` or `pRefPoint` specified. In older versions of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM.com / ESI versions), the solver expects these to be set explicitly when using PIMPLE/PISO with pressure fields.

## Solution

Add either `pRefCell` or `pRefPoint` to the `fvSolution` dictionary. Since the pressure field is being solved via the `PCG`/`PBiCGStab` solvers, adding `pRefCell` is the simpler approach.

**Add the following entries to the `fvSolution` file** (inside the main dictionary, alongside `solvers`, `PIMPLE`, and `PISO`):

```
pRefCell        0;
pRefValue       0;
```

Alternatively, you could use `pRefPoint` instead:

```
pRefPoint       (0.5 0.5 0);
```

Either approach will work. The `pRefCell` method is more common for `pisoFoam`.

So the complete `fvSolution` would have three sections: `solvers`, `PIMPLE`, `PISO`, and then the two new lines `pRefCell 0;` and `pRefValue 0;` at the same level as those sections.

No other files need modification. This is consistent with the reference cavity case pattern where pressure anchoring is required for the Poisson solve.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSolution', 'changes': 'Add pRefCell 0; after the PISO sub-dictionary entry; Add pRefValue 0; after pRefCell line'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do an incompressible lid driven cavity flow using pisoFoam. The cavity is a square with dimensions normalized to 1 unit on both the x and y axes and very thin in the z-direction (0.1 units) and entire domain scaled down by a factor of 0.1 making it effectively 2D. Use a grid of 20X20 in x and y direction and 1 cell in z-direction(due to the expected 2D flow characteristics). The top wall ('movingWall') moves in the x-direction with a uniform velocity of 1 m/s. The 'fixedWalls' have a no-slip boundary condition (velocity equal to zero at the wall). The front and back faces are designated as 'empty'. The simulation runs from time 0 to 10 with a time step of 0.005 units, and results are output every 100 time steps. The viscosity (`nu`) is set as constant with a value of 1e-05 m^2/s. use k epsilon urbulence model.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
